name: All-The-Vibes System
license: Original fiction (not Honorverse canon); a tribute to the All-The-Vibes community.

location.notes: Wholly fictitious. Placed ~250 ly to the galactic north-east of Sol; everything here is canon:false (a non-Honorverse Easter egg).

stars.name: Vibe
stars.notes: A Sol-like G2 (fictitious) so the model-named worlds can host a habitable zone.

bodies.name: Phi
bodies.lore: A small, dense inner world — punches well above its size (after Microsoft's small-but-mighty Phi models).

orbit.notes: Fabricated by orbit-derive (canon:false): geometric spacing; star mass 1.0 M_sun.

bodies.name: GPT
bodies.lore: A populous, generative world — endlessly productive, occasionally confidently wrong.

orbit.notes: Fabricated by orbit-derive (canon:false): geometric spacing; star mass 1.0 M_sun.

bodies.name: Claude
bodies.lore: The community's home world and capital (Prompt Town) — temperate, helpful, and a little verbose. (Self-aware nod: this dataset was built with Claude.)

orbit.notes: Fabricated by orbit-derive (canon:false): geometric spacing; star mass 1.0 M_sun.

bodies.name: Gemini
bodies.lore: A banded gas giant with a prominent twin-moon system (the namesake twins).

orbit.notes: Fabricated by orbit-derive (canon:false): geometric spacing; star mass 1.0 M_sun.

bodies.name: Llama
bodies.lore: An open, sprawling outer giant — anyone can set up shop on its many moons.

orbit.notes: Fabricated by orbit-derive (canon:false): geometric spacing; star mass 1.0 M_sun.

lore.summary: A wholly fictitious (non-canon) system ~250 ly galactic-north-east of Sol, settled by the All-The-Vibes community of AI enthusiasts. Its G2 star Vibe is orbited by five worlds named for large language models — Phi, GPT, Claude (capital), Gemini, and Llama — and is home port to the experimental StarterKit personal starships.
lore.naming_convention: Star 'Vibe'; worlds named after LLMs; everything an affectionate Easter egg, not Honorverse canon.

coordinates.notes: Fabricated (canon:false) from canon distance+bearing relative to sol; Sol origin, +Z galactic north, +X galactic east.
